rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring all property definitions and constants are properly declared in the AADL model or its dependencies before use in AGREE contracts.
Resolve type errors in AGREE expressions by ensuring all identifiers and helper declarations refer to valid AADL features or previously declared AGREE helpers with matching types.
When AGREE reports 'named thing must be an expression with a type', remove or correct the invalid clause instead of adding new architecture elements.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference identifiers that are visible in the current scope or explicitly declared within the same annex.
Ensure all expressions in assume/guarantee clauses have consistent types; avoid mixing int, real, and bool without explicit conversion.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in const and eq declarations to prevent resolution errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE clauses must be placed in valid scopes: assumptions and guarantees in component types, assigns in implementations.
Property references in AGREE must resolve to definitions present in the AADL model or configured dependencies.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new property definitions or constants in AGREE annexes that are not already declared in the AADL model.
Do not attempt to fix type mismatches by adding architecture elements; instead, correct the expression or helper declaration.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
Component type vs implementation scope rules for contracts
AGREE annex placement and visibility rules
